headline: US export controls on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 were lifted after a two-week freeze, restoring access to the frontier models.
body: The dominant story this quarter is the reversal of US export controls on Anthropic's most advanced models, with the White House and Department of Commerce dropping restrictions on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after a roughly two-week ban and Anthropic pledging to restore access. New model releases including Sonnet 5 and Fable 5 arrived alongside this policy shift, reopening the frontier-model landscape. On the tooling side, breakouts cluster around AI-assisted development, with open weights and HNSW breaking out and spec-driven development, debugging, deterministic behavior, event logging, and AST-aware chunking entering as new techniques. Wayve's $85M employee tender at an $8.5B valuation and Vinton Cerf's retirement from Google round out the window.

cited_items.title: The “Father of the Internet” is finally retiring
White House drops restrictions on Anthropic AI models after two-week ban - The Washington Post
Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
Wayve launches $85M employee tender offer at $8.5B valuation
US lifts curbs on Anthropic's Fable, Mythos AI models
